zh.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London,: 英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Stoner於1445年出生在英國奧可福爾斯頓時，他的父親Thomas Stonor II21歲，母親Joan de la Pole15歲。他於1481年與Anne Neville結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他在英國奧可福爾被列為sheriff 。他去世於1494年5月21日在他的家鄉享年49歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Stoner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Sussex): topographic name from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone’ + -er, used for someone who lived by a prominent rock or stone, or perhaps who came from a place called Stone, although this formation is more rare. See Stone and compare Stoneman .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Steiner  .

variants.name: Stone
meanings: English: from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone, rock’ (Old English stān). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on stony ground, by a notable outcrop of rock, or by a stone boundary-marker or monument, or habitational, from a place called Stone, such as those in Buckinghamshire, Devon, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Staffordshire, and Worcestershire.
Irish (Kilkenny): adopted for Irish Ó Clochartaigh (see Clougherty ) and/or Ó Clochasaigh (see Clohessy ), and possibly several other names containing or thought to contain the element cloch ‘stone’.
Americanized form (translation into English) of various surnames in other languages, meaning ‘stone’, including Jewish Stein , Norwegian Steine, French Lapierre .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Stoner was born in 1445, in Stonor, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Thomas Stonor II, was 21 and his mother, Joan de la Pole, was 15. He married Anne Neville in 1481. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. His occupation is listed as sheriff  in Oxfordshire, England. He died on 21 May 1494, in his hometown, at the age of 49, and was buried in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Stoner est né en 1445, à Stonor, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Stonor II, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Joan de la Pole, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Anne Neville en 1481. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff  à Oxfordshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 21 mai 1494, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Stoner 1445 in Stonor, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Stonor II 21 und seine Mutter Joan de la Pole 15. Er hat 1481 Anne Neville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. sein Beruf ist als sheriff  in Oxfordshire, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 21. Mai 1494 im Alter von 49 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Anne Neville nel 1481. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. La sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff  nel Oxfordshire, England. Morì il 21 maggio 1494, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolto a Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London,: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Stoner(이)가 1445년 영국 스퍼드실르 스토너에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Stonor II(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de la Pole(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1481년 Anne Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 스퍼드실르에서 sheriff 이었다. 그는 1494년 5월 21일 그의 고향에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Stoner nasceu em 1445, em Stonor, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Stonor II, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Joan de la Pole, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne Neville em 1481. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Sua profissão aparece como sheriff  em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 21 de maio de 1494, em sua cidade natal, com 49 anos, e foi sepultado em Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London,: вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Stoner родился в 1445 г. в местности стонор, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Stonor II было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Joan de la Pole, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Neville в 1481 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь.  Он работал по профессии sheriff  в местности оксфордшир, Англия. Он умер 21 мая 1494 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
